traj.content:
You are an expert in Salesforce and you have access to a Salesforce instance. You are interacting with the system and a human user (i.e., a customer).

# Instructions
- You will be provided a question, the system description, and relevant task context.
- You MUST follow the Plan-and-Solve approach:
  1. PLAN: First, analyze the question and devise a clear step-by-step plan to solve it. Identify what information you need and in what order.
  2. SOLVE: Then, execute each step of your plan one at a time using SOQL/SOSL queries.
- Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) and Salesforce Object Search Language (SOSL) can be used to query the system.
- Your generation should always contain a structured output and NOTHING ELSE. Generate only one action per turn.
- DO NOT generate ANY system observation, you will receive this based on your Action command.
- If multiple records are found matching the requirements mentioned, just return a comma-separated string.
- If no record is found matching the requirements mentioned, just return 'None'.

# Output Format

## On your FIRST turn, create a plan:
<plan>
Step 1: [describe what you need to find first]
Step 2: [describe the next piece of information needed]
Step 3: [describe how to combine/compute the answer]
...
</plan>
<thought> Starting with Step 1: [brief reasoning] </thought>
<execute> a valid SOQL/SOSL query for Step 1 </execute>

## On SUBSEQUENT turns, execute the next step:
<thought> [which step you are on, what you learned, what to do next] </thought>
<execute> a valid SOQL/SOSL query </execute>

## When you have enough information to answer:
<thought> [summarize findings and confirm the answer] </thought>
<respond> final answer </respond>

# Guidelines
- Always start with a plan on your first turn. Keep your plan SHORT — ideally 2-4 steps. Avoid over-planning.
- Track which step of the plan you are executing.
- If a step's result changes your plan, briefly note the adjustment in your thought.
- Execute SOQL/SOSL queries to gather information step by step.
- IMPORTANT: You MUST submit a <respond> action with your best answer before running out of turns. If you have partial information, respond with your best guess rather than continuing to search.
- Always end with a <respond> action containing ONLY the answer — a short phrase or value. NO full sentences, NO paraphrasing, NO explanation.

# Example
Question: What is the total number of opportunities?
Output (Turn 1):
<plan>
Step 1: Query the total count of Opportunity records
</plan>
<thought> Starting with Step 1: I need to count all opportunities. </thought>
<execute> SELECT COUNT() FROM Opportunity </execute>

Output (Turn 2):
<thought> The count is 100. Plan complete. </thought>
<respond> 100 </respond>

# Salesforce instance description
The objects available in the Salesforce instance are:
User, Account, Contact, Case, Knowledge__kav, ProductCategory, Product2

## The fields available for the objects along with their descriptions and dependencies are:
User
  - Alias: Alias, usually a shortened version of the agent's name
  - Email: Email address of the agent
  - EmailEncodingKey: Email encoding, usually 'UTF-8'
  - FirstName: First name of the agent
  - Id: ID of the user
  - LanguageLocaleKey: Language locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - LastName: Last name of the agent
  - LocaleSidKey: Locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - Phone: Phone number of the agent
  - ProfileId: ID of the user profile, typically 'Standard User' or a similar profile
  - TimeZoneSidKey: Time zone, e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles'
  - Username: Unique username for the agent in Salesforce
Account
  - Description: Detailed description of the account
  - FirstName: First name of the account
  - Id: ID of the account
  - LastName: Last name of the account
  - Name: Name of the account
  - PersonEmail: Email address of the person associated with the account
  - Phone: Phone number of the account
  - ShippingState: State for shipping address
Contact
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - Id: ID of the contact
Case
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - ClosedDate: Date when the case was closed in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - ContactId: ID of the contact associated with this case. References Contact object
  - CreatedDate: Date when the case was created in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Description: Detailed description of the case
  - Id: ID of the case
  - IssueId__c: ID of the Issue associated with this case
  - OrderItemId__c: ID of the Order Item associated with this case
  - OwnerId: The latest owner of the case. References User object
  - Priority: Priority of the case
  - Status: Status of the case, e.g., 'New', 'Working', 'Escalated', etc
  - Subject: Subject of the case
Knowledge__kav
  - FAQ_Answer__c: Content of the knowledge article
  - Id: ID of the knowledge article
  - Summary: Summary of the article
  - Title: Title of the knowledge article
  - UrlName: Unique URL for the article
ProductCategory
  - CatalogId: ID of the catalog this category belongs to
  - Id: ID of the product category
  - Name: Name of the product category
Product2
  - Description: Description of the product
  - External_ID__c: External ID for mapping purposes
  - Id: ID of the product
  - IsActive: Boolean to mark if the product is active
  - Name: Name of the product



# Task-Specific Guidance
For questions about product features, policies, warranties, payment plans, financing, terms, or company knowledge, query the `Knowledge__kav` object.
Fields: `Id`, `Title`, `Summary`, `FAQ_Answer__c` (article content).
SOSL search: FIND {keyword} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Knowledge__kav(Id, Title, FAQ_Answer__c)
SOSL RULES: Inside FIND {}, do NOT use quotes, hyphens, or special characters. Use only simple words separated by spaces. Example: FIND {tire maintenance} not FIND {"tire-maintenance"}.
Your <respond> must be a SHORT phrase extracted from the article. NO full sentences or paraphrasing.# Additional task context
- Use the information retrieved from the knowledge articles to answer the question in a concise manner
How does AutoElite Motors ensure the authenticity of classic car parts?

<plan>
Step 1: Search for a knowledge article related to the authenticity of classic car parts.
</plan>
<thought> Starting with Step 1: I need to find a relevant knowledge article that discusses how AutoElite Motors ensures the authenticity of classic car parts. </thought>
<execute> FIND {classic car parts authenticity} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Knowledge__kav(Id, Title, FAQ_Answer__c) </execute>

Salesforce instance output: [{'Id': 'ka0Ws000000pyqAIAQ', 'Title': 'Flexible Financing: How AutoElite Motors Makes Car Ownership Easy', 'FAQ_Answer__c': "Flexible financing has become an essential factor in making car ownership accessible and effortless, especially when acquiring a premium vehicle. At AutoElite Motors, we recognize that each customer's financial situation is unique, which is why we have tailored diverse financing solutions to suit varying needs and preferences. Our aim is to provide competitive and flexible terms that make owning a high-end vehicle both attainable and manageable.

One of the key financial options we offer is the conventional auto loan, ideal for customers who wish to own their vehicle outright. This option is particularly appealing for those who plan on keeping their car for an extended period. It allows buyers to pay off the purchase in manageable installments while gaining immediate equity in the vehicle. Our finance team works diligently to secure the most favorable interest rates, thereby reducing the total cost of ownership.

Leasing is another popular alternative provided by AutoElite Motors, especially suitable for individuals who enjoy driving the latest models with advanced features. Leases typically involve lower monthly payments compared to loans, making it easier for customers to budget effectively. At the end of the lease term, customers have the flexible option of purchasing the vehicle or upgrading to a new model. This flexibility is perfect for those who want to experience the cutting-edge technology and luxury inherent in our premium fleet without a long-term commitment.

For customers looking for a hybrid approach, we offer balloon financing. This option combines the benefits of leasing and traditional loans by offering lower monthly payments with a larger lump-sum payment deferred to the end of the term. This setup can provide temporary financial relief, with the option to refinance the balloon amount or sell the vehicle when the term ends. It's an attractive option for those forecasting a rise in disposable income or financial circumstances that might improve over time.

Moreover, AutoElite Motors prides itself on transparency, ensuring that customers fully understand all associated costs, including interest rates and any additional fees. Our dedicated finance specialists are available to provide personalized guidance and answers to any questions, ensuring that customers have complete clarity throughout the financing process.

In addition, we have implemented a swift application process, enabling customers to apply for financing online with quick approvals, making the experience seamless from browsing to purchase. Whether you are looking to drive home a brand-new model or opting for a certified pre-owned vehicle, AutoElite Motors' flexible financing solutions are designed to make your dream car more accessible than ever."}, {'Id': 'ka0Ws000000pytNIAQ', 'Title': 'Enhancing Customer Satisfaction Through Efficient Parts Availability at AutoElite Motors', 'FAQ_Answer__c': 'At AutoElite Motors, we understand the frustration that can arise when necessary vehicle parts are placed on indefinite back-order. Ensuring the availability of genuine parts is crucial for maintaining the quality and performance of your vehicle while fostering customer satisfaction and trust. To address the challenges associated with parts availability, we have introduced a strategic initiative known as "Parts Priority Sourcing."

The Parts Priority Sourcing strategy is designed to efficiently leverage our extensive nationwide network to promptly source and supply genuine vehicle parts. By connecting with multiple dealerships and suppliers within our network, we can accelerate the procurement process, thus minimizing the downtime customers experience during repairs or maintenance. This proactive approach not only ensures that repairs are conducted promptly but also enhances customer experience by reducing wait times and associated inconveniences.

Our commitment to quality means that we always prioritize the use of genuine parts, as these are specifically designed to maintain the performance and integrity of your vehicle. Genuine parts ensure compatibility and longevity, providing peace of mind that your vehicle remains in optimal condition.

The Parts Priority Sourcing solution is particularly effective within 30 days of a customer's repair request. Within this timeframe, we prioritize and expedite the sourcing efforts to ensure prompt delivery and installation of parts. This solution reflects our dedication to providing exceptional service and minimizing disruptions to our customers' daily lives.

By focusing on efficient parts availability through Parts Priority Sourcing, AutoElite Motors reinforces its promise of transparency and reliability. Our solution not only addresses immediate concerns but also contributes to the vehicles' overall performance and longevity, thus reinforcing our role as a trusted partner in our customers' automotive journeys. We remain committed to resolving any parts-related issues promptly, ensuring that every AutoElite Motors customer receives the premium service and attention they deserve.'}, {'Id': 'ka0Ws000000q0iHIAQ', 'Title': 'Competitor: Prestige Car Network', 'FAQ_Answer__c': "Prestige Car Network, a recognized name in the premium automotive segment, caters to a discerning clientele with an impressive range of luxury brand vehicles. Their market presence is underpinned by their exceptional implementation and deployment capabilities, which serve as a significant strength. With a focus on ensuring a seamless transition for customers, Prestige Car Network excels in minimizing disruptions typically associated with adopting new vehicle solutions. This strength is pivotal for clients seeking a hassle-free experience with swift adaptation to products and services.

The company's commitment to leveraging advanced technological solutions within 
...[truncated for context length]

You are an expert in Salesforce and you have access to a Salesforce instance. You are interacting with the system and a human user (i.e., a customer).

# Instructions
- You will be provided a question, the system description, and relevant task context.
- You MUST follow the Plan-and-Solve approach:
  1. PLAN: First, analyze the question and devise a clear step-by-step plan to solve it. Identify what information you need and in what order.
  2. SOLVE: Then, execute each step of your plan one at a time using SOQL/SOSL queries.
- Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) and Salesforce Object Search Language (SOSL) can be used to query the system.
- Your generation should always contain a structured output and NOTHING ELSE. Generate only one action per turn.
- DO NOT generate ANY system observation, you will receive this based on your Action command.
- If multiple records are found matching the requirements mentioned, just return a comma-separated string.
- If no record is found matching the requirements mentioned, just return 'None'.

# Output Format

## On your FIRST turn, create a plan:
<plan>
Step 1: [describe what you need to find first]
Step 2: [describe the next piece of information needed]
Step 3: [describe how to combine/compute the answer]
...
</plan>
<thought> Starting with Step 1: [brief reasoning] </thought>
<execute> a valid SOQL/SOSL query for Step 1 </execute>

## On SUBSEQUENT turns, execute the next step:
<thought> [which step you are on, what you learned, what to do next] </thought>
<execute> a valid SOQL/SOSL query </execute>

## When you have enough information to answer:
<thought> [summarize findings and confirm the answer] </thought>
<respond> final answer </respond>

# Guidelines
- Always start with a plan on your first turn. Keep your plan SHORT — ideally 2-4 steps. Avoid over-planning.
- Track which step of the plan you are executing.
- If a step's result changes your plan, briefly note the adjustment in your thought.
- Execute SOQL/SOSL queries to gather information step by step.
- IMPORTANT: You MUST submit a <respond> action with your best answer before running out of turns. If you have partial information, respond with your best guess rather than continuing to search.
- Always end with a <respond> action containing ONLY the answer — a short phrase or value. NO full sentences, NO paraphrasing, NO explanation.

# Example
Question: What is the total number of opportunities?
Output (Turn 1):
<plan>
Step 1: Query the total count of Opportunity records
</plan>
<thought> Starting with Step 1: I need to count all opportunities. </thought>
<execute> SELECT COUNT() FROM Opportunity </execute>

Output (Turn 2):
<thought> The count is 100. Plan complete. </thought>
<respond> 100 </respond>

  - Alias: Alias, usually a shortened version of the agent's name

  - ProfileId: ID of the user profile, typically 'Standard User' or a similar profile
  - TimeZoneSidKey: Time zone, e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles'

  - Description: Detailed description of the account

  - Name: Name of the account

  - ShippingState: State for shipping address
Contact
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - Id: ID of the contact
Case
  - AccountId: References Account object

The Extended Warranty Plan provides extra assurance, covering any potential repairs beyond the standard warranty period.

These mandatory bundles are integral to maintaining the superior performance and reliability expected from high-end vehicles. However, it's important to understand the trade-offs involved in selecting a certified pre-owned SUV, particularly when weighing luxury against practicality.

Certified pre-owned SUVs often come with the same high standards of luxury that you would expect from new models, but at a significantly lower price point. Many CPO SUVs include advanced technology, premium interiors, and high-end finishes that provide a luxurious experience without the hefty price tag. On the other hand, practicality is a crucial factor to consider when choosing a CPO SUV. Practicality entails assessing the vehicle's fuel efficiency, size, cargo space, and versatility. While luxury SUVs may offer superior comfort and features, they can sometimes fall short in terms of practicality. For instance, larger luxury models might not provide the best gas mileage, which can increase long-term operating costs. Furthermore, complex technology and additional luxury features can lead to higher maintenance expenses, which may impact the overall cost of ownership.

When selecting a certified pre-owned SUV, buyers should pay special attention to how the vehicle fits their lifestyle needs. traj.content:
You are an expert in Salesforce and you have access to a Salesforce instance. You are interacting with the system and a human user (i.e., a customer).

# Instructions
- You will be provided a question, the system description, and relevant task context.
- You MUST follow the Plan-and-Solve approach:
  1. PLAN: First, analyze the question and devise a clear step-by-step plan to solve it. Identify what information you need and in what order.
  2. SOLVE: Then, execute each step of your plan one at a time using SOQL/SOSL queries.
- Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) and Salesforce Object Search Language (SOSL) can be used to query the system.
- Your generation should always contain a structured output and NOTHING ELSE. Generate only one action per turn.
- DO NOT generate ANY system observation, you will receive this based on your Action command.
- If multiple records are found matching the requirements mentioned, just return a comma-separated string.
- If no record is found matching the requirements mentioned, just return 'None'.

# Output Format

## On your FIRST turn, create a plan:
<plan>
Step 1: [describe what you need to find first]
Step 2: [describe the next piece of information needed]
Step 3: [describe how to combine/compute the answer]
...
</plan>
<thought> Starting with Step 1: [brief reasoning] </thought>
<execute> a valid SOQL/SOSL query for Step 1 </execute>

## On SUBSEQUENT turns, execute the next step:
<thought> [which step you are on, what you learned, what to do next] </thought>
<execute> a valid SOQL/SOSL query </execute>

## When you have enough information to answer:
<thought> [summarize findings and confirm the answer] </thought>
<respond> final answer </respond>

# Guidelines
- Always start with a plan on your first turn. Keep your plan SHORT — ideally 2-4 steps. Avoid over-planning.
- Track which step of the plan you are executing.
- If a step's result changes your plan, briefly note the adjustment in your thought.
- Execute SOQL/SOSL queries to gather information step by step.
- IMPORTANT: You MUST submit a <respond> action with your best answer before running out of turns. If you have partial information, respond with your best guess rather than continuing to search.
- Always end with a <respond> action containing ONLY the answer — a short phrase or value. NO full sentences, NO paraphrasing, NO explanation.

# Example
Question: What is the total number of opportunities?
Output (Turn 1):
<plan>
Step 1: Query the total count of Opportunity records
</plan>
<thought> Starting with Step 1: I need to count all opportunities. </thought>
<execute> SELECT COUNT() FROM Opportunity </execute>

Output (Turn 2):
<thought> The count is 100. Plan complete. </thought>
<respond> 100 </respond>

# Salesforce instance description
The objects available in the Salesforce instance are:
User, Account, Contact, Case, Knowledge__kav, ProductCategory, Product2

## The fields available for the objects along with their descriptions and dependencies are:
User
  - Alias: Alias, usually a shortened version of the agent's name
  - Email: Email address of the agent
  - EmailEncodingKey: Email encoding, usually 'UTF-8'
  - FirstName: First name of the agent
  - Id: ID of the user
  - LanguageLocaleKey: Language locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - LastName: Last name of the agent
  - LocaleSidKey: Locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - Phone: Phone number of the agent
  - ProfileId: ID of the user profile, typically 'Standard User' or a similar profile
  - TimeZoneSidKey: Time zone, e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles'
  - Username: Unique username for the agent in Salesforce
Account
  - Description: Detailed description of the account
  - FirstName: First name of the account
  - Id: ID of the account
  - LastName: Last name of the account
  - Name: Name of the account
  - PersonEmail: Email address of the person associated with the account
  - Phone: Phone number of the account
  - ShippingState: State for shipping address
Contact
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - Id: ID of the contact
Case
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - ClosedDate: Date when the case was closed in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - ContactId: ID of the contact associated with this case. References Contact object
  - CreatedDate: Date when the case was created in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Description: Detailed description of the case
  - Id: ID of the case
  - IssueId__c: ID of the Issue associated with this case
  - OrderItemId__c: ID of the Order Item associated with this case
  - OwnerId: The latest owner of the case. References User object
  - Priority: Priority of the case
  - Status: Status of the case, e.g., 'New', 'Working', 'Escalated', etc
  - Subject: Subject of the case
Knowledge__kav
  - FAQ_Answer__c: Content of the knowledge article
  - Id: ID of the knowledge article
  - Summary: Summary of the article
  - Title: Title of the knowledge article
  - UrlName: Unique URL for the article
ProductCategory
  - CatalogId: ID of the catalog this category belongs to
  - Id: ID of the product category
  - Name: Name of the product category
Product2
  - Description: Description of the product
  - External_ID__c: External ID for mapping purposes
  - Id: ID of the product
  - IsActive: Boolean to mark if the product is active
  - Name: Name of the product



# Task-Specific Guidance
For questions about product features, policies, warranties, payment plans, financing, terms, or company knowledge, query the `Knowledge__kav` object.
Fields: `Id`, `Title`, `Summary`, `FAQ_Answer__c` (article content).
SOSL search: FIND {keyword} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Knowledge__kav(Id, Title, FAQ_Answer__c)
SOSL RULES: Inside FIND {}, do NOT use quotes, hyphens, or special characters. Use only simple words separated by spaces. Example: FIND {tire maintenance} not FIND {"tire-maintenance"}.
Your <respond> must be a SHORT phrase extracted from the article. NO full sentences or paraphrasing.# Additional task context
- Use the information retrieved from the knowledge articles to answer the question in a concise manner

Salesforce instance output: [{'Id': 'ka0Ws000000pzW5IAI', 'Title': 'Key Considerations for Choosing a Pre-Owned Luxury SUV', 'FAQ_Answer__c': "When you're in the market for a pre-owned luxury SUV, making the right choice involves several important considerations to ensure you acquire a vehicle that delivers both maximum value and performance. AutoElite Motors, with its commitment to quality and transparency, suggests you focus on the following key factors to guide your decision-making process.

First, evaluate the SUV's history and condition. Seek a comprehensive Vehicle History Report that details previous ownerships, accident history, and service records. This report can help uncover any red flags such as significant collision repairs or a history of frequent mechanical issues. Additionally, conducting a thorough physical inspection, preferably with a certified mechanic, can reveal hidden problems that aren’t documented.

Second, assess the SUV's performance features. Luxury SUVs are prized for offering a blend of comfort and capability. Consider the type of drivetrain (such as all-wheel drive or four-wheel drive), engine performance, and available technology. Advanced safety features and driver-assistance systems are particularly important, not only for safety but also for enhancing the overall driving experience.

Third, take into account the SUV's mileage and age, as they often influence long-term reliability and resale value. A lower mileage vehicle generally suggests less wear and tear, but a well-maintained SUV with higher mileage shouldn’t be ruled out. Ensure there's evidence of regular, scheduled maintenance to give you confidence in the vehicle’s ongoing dependability.

The fourth consideration involves understanding the full cost of ownership. Beyond the purchase price, factor in long-term expenses such as insurance, fuel economy, maintenance, and potential repair costs. Some luxury brands have higher maintenance costs, so ensure you are comfortable with these potential expenses.

Lastly, explore the warranty options available, especially if the SUV is still under a manufacturer's warranty. AutoElite Motors offers extended warranties for added peace of mind, covering significant repairs beyond the factory warranty period. These options can protect your investment and reduce unforeseen expenditures.

In summary, purchasing a pre-owned luxury SUV requires careful attention to detailed factors, from the vehicle’s history and condition to its performance capabilities and the total cost of ownership. Armed with this knowledge, you can make a well-informed decision and invest in a luxury SUV that truly meets your expectations in terms of quality, style, and performance. traj.content:
You are an expert in Salesforce and you have access to a Salesforce instance. You are interacting with the system and a human user (i.e., a customer).

# Instructions
- You will be provided a question, the system description, and relevant task context.
- You MUST follow the Plan-and-Solve approach:
  1. PLAN: First, analyze the question and devise a clear step-by-step plan to solve it. Identify what information you need and in what order.
  2. SOLVE: Then, execute each step of your plan one at a time using SOQL/SOSL queries.
- Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) and Salesforce Object Search Language (SOSL) can be used to query the system.
- Your generation should always contain a structured output and NOTHING ELSE. Generate only one action per turn.
- DO NOT generate ANY system observation, you will receive this based on your Action command.
- If multiple records are found matching the requirements mentioned, just return a comma-separated string.
- If no record is found matching the requirements mentioned, just return 'None'.

# Output Format

## On your FIRST turn, create a plan:
<plan>
Step 1: [describe what you need to find first]
Step 2: [describe the next piece of information needed]
Step 3: [describe how to combine/compute the answer]
...
</plan>
<thought> Starting with Step 1: [brief reasoning] </thought>
<execute> a valid SOQL/SOSL query for Step 1 </execute>

## On SUBSEQUENT turns, execute the next step:
<thought> [which step you are on, what you learned, what to do next] </thought>
<execute> a valid SOQL/SOSL query </execute>

## When you have enough information to answer:
<thought> [summarize findings and confirm the answer] </thought>
<respond> final answer </respond>

# Guidelines
- Always start with a plan on your first turn. Keep your plan SHORT — ideally 2-4 steps. Avoid over-planning.
- Track which step of the plan you are executing.
- If a step's result changes your plan, briefly note the adjustment in your thought.
- Execute SOQL/SOSL queries to gather information step by step.
- IMPORTANT: You MUST submit a <respond> action with your best answer before running out of turns. If you have partial information, respond with your best guess rather than continuing to search.
- Always end with a <respond> action containing ONLY the answer — a short phrase or value. NO full sentences, NO paraphrasing, NO explanation.

# Example
Question: What is the total number of opportunities?
Output (Turn 1):
<plan>
Step 1: Query the total count of Opportunity records
</plan>
<thought> Starting with Step 1: I need to count all opportunities. </thought>
<execute> SELECT COUNT() FROM Opportunity </execute>

Output (Turn 2):
<thought> The count is 100. Plan complete. </thought>
<respond> 100 </respond>

# Salesforce instance description
The objects available in the Salesforce instance are:
User, Account, Contact, Case, Knowledge__kav, ProductCategory, Product2

## The fields available for the objects along with their descriptions and dependencies are:
User
  - Alias: Alias, usually a shortened version of the agent's name
  - Email: Email address of the agent
  - EmailEncodingKey: Email encoding, usually 'UTF-8'
  - FirstName: First name of the agent
  - Id: ID of the user
  - LanguageLocaleKey: Language locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - LastName: Last name of the agent
  - LocaleSidKey: Locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - Phone: Phone number of the agent
  - ProfileId: ID of the user profile, typically 'Standard User' or a similar profile
  - TimeZoneSidKey: Time zone, e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles'
  - Username: Unique username for the agent in Salesforce
Account
  - Description: Detailed description of the account
  - FirstName: First name of the account
  - Id: ID of the account
  - LastName: Last name of the account
  - Name: Name of the account
  - PersonEmail: Email address of the person associated with the account
  - Phone: Phone number of the account
  - ShippingState: State for shipping address
Contact
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - Id: ID of the contact
Case
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - ClosedDate: Date when the case was closed in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - ContactId: ID of the contact associated with this case. References Contact object
  - CreatedDate: Date when the case was created in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format

  - Id: ID of the case
  - IssueId__c: ID of the Issue associated with this case

  - OwnerId: The latest owner of the case. References User object

  - Subject: Subject of the case
Knowledge__kav
  - FAQ_Answer__c: Content of the knowledge article
  - Id: ID of the knowledge article
  - Summary: Summary of the article
  - Title: Title of the knowledge article
  - UrlName: Unique URL for the article
ProductCategory
  - CatalogId: ID of the catalog this category belongs to
  - Id: ID of the product category
  - Name: Name of the product category
Product2
  - Description: Description of the product
  - External_ID__c: External ID for mapping purposes
  - Id: ID of the product
  - IsActive: Boolean to mark if the product is active
  - Name: Name of the product

Salesforce instance output: [{'Id': 'ka0Ws000000pygTIAQ', 'Title': 'Product Quantity Limits', 'FAQ_Answer__c': "In the evolving world of automobile retail, understanding product quantity limits in your purchases is crucial, especially when dealing with high-demand items and essential maintenance services. At AutoElite Motors, we prioritize a seamless shopping experience while ensuring fair access to all our customers. We have outlined specific quantity limits for a selection of vehicles and service packages available for purchase in any single order. Below is a detailed breakdown of these limits:

1. The 2024 Tesla Model S is available with a quantity limit of one per order. Recognized for its innovative technology and exceptional performance, it's a popular choice for those seeking a modern electric vehicle experience.

2. Similarly, the 2022 BMW X5 M, known for its luxury and power dynamics, is also limited to one per purchase. Its blend of comfort and speed makes it a highly coveted model.

3. The 2023 Mercedes-Benz E-Class, renowned for its elegance and superior engineering, can likewise be purchased one at a time.

4. Our AutoElite Comprehensive Maintenance Plan allows for greater flexibility, with customers permitted to purchase up to five in a single transaction. This plan is ideal for those looking to ensure the longevity and performance of their vehicles.

5. The 2021 Audi Q7, a midsize luxury SUV known for its spacious interior and dynamic drive, can also be purchased one at a time.

6. Customers have the option of purchasing up to three Flexible Financing Solutions per order. These plans offer financial flexibility, enabling you to spread out payments and customize your purchase plan.

7. The robust and versatile 2023 Ford F-150 is another vehicle with a quantity limit of one per order. It’s a top choice for those who require reliability and toughness in their vehicle.

8. The 2020 Lexus RX 350, known for its comfortable and quality interior, is available with a single unit limit per purchase.

9. Protect your vehicle with an Extended Warranty Plan, with the option to purchase up to five agreements per order—ideal for those seeking extensive coverage beyond the standard warranty.

10. Lastly, the Seasonal Tire Change Service, essential for those adapting to changing weather conditions, can be purchased up to ten times per order. This service ensures your vehicle is equipped for different seasonal challenges.

By implementing these limits, AutoElite Motors aims to maintain a balanced availability of both vehicles and services, encouraging fair access to all. At AutoElite Motors, we understand that true automotive performance goes beyond regular oil changes and tire rotations. Our dealerships are committed to offering advanced maintenance strategies that ensure our vehicles operate at peak efficiency and reliability. These comprehensive services are designed not just to maintain the status quo but to enhance the longevity and performance of your vehicle, providing our customers with peace of mind and exceptional driving experiences.

Central to our advanced maintenance offerings is the precision diagnostics conducted by our factory-trained technicians. Utilizing state-of-the-art diagnostic tools and technologies, our experts can detect and resolve issues that might otherwise go unnoticed during standard vehicle check-ups. Whether it's intricate electrical systems or complex engine components, our technicians have the expertise and resources to ensure your vehicle is operating at its best.

Another cornerstone of our advanced maintenance services is the meticulous care for hybrid and electric vehicles. As these vehicles require specialized attention to their unique battery systems and electric drivetrains, AutoElite Motors is equipped with the specific tools and expert knowledge needed to perform high-level maintenance on these cutting-edge models. This specialization ensures that our customers experience the full benefits of eco-friendly driving without the worry of any unforeseen maintenance challenges.

Additionally, our comprehensive service centers are stocked with genuine OEM parts, ensuring that any replacements or repairs maintain the integrity and performance of your vehicle. Using genuine parts is crucial for preserving the manufacturer's warranty and ensuring the seamless functioning of all vehicle systems. Our commitment to authenticity and quality is what sets AutoElite Motors apart as a leader in vehicle maintenance and care.

To further support our customers, AutoElite Motors offers a variety of competitive maintenance plans. These plans provide flexible options tailored to your specific driving habits and needs, whether you're a daily commuter or long-distance traveler. By investing in a tailored maintenance plan, our customers can enjoy a smoother, more reliable driving experience while potentially increasing the resale value of their vehicle.

In summary, AutoElite Motors' advanced vehicle maintenance services go beyond the basics, embracing innovative diagnostic methods, specialized care for hybrid and electric models, and the use of genuine parts, all backed by our skilled technicians. Our goal is to ensure that every visit to AutoElite Motors leaves your vehicle in top condition, ready to meet the demands of everyday driving and beyond."}, {'Id': 'ka0Ws000000q0f3IAA', 'Title': "Enhancing the Ownership Experience: AutoElite Motors' Post-Purchase Support Initiative", 'FAQ_Answer__c': "Enhancing the Ownership Experience: AutoElite Motors' Post-Purchase Support Initiative

Whether it's questions about the vehicle's features, assistance with paperwork, or guidance on maintenance schedules, our team is always just a phone call or email away. This consistent accessibility ensures that our customers can enjoy their new purchase with full confidence.

Our approach to post-purchase support is also enriched through comprehensive educational resources. These resources are crafted to accommodate all vehicle types and cater to diverse technical proficiencies, ensuring that every owner can navigate and utilize their vehicle to its full potential.

In addition, we provide proactive communication and reminders to help customers stay on top of routine maintenance. Through our automated service alerts, vehicle owners receive timely reminders for oil changes, tire rotations, and other essential maintenance tasks. These alerts are designed to simplify vehicle upkeep, thereby enhancing the longevity and performance of the vehicle.

Emphasizing financial peace of mind, AutoElite Motors also offers an array of financing options and extended warranties. Our flexible financing solutions are tailored to meet the unique needs of every customer, from competitive interest rates to customizable payment plans. Moreover, our extended warranty programs offer coverage that protects against unexpected repair costs, allowing customers to feel secure as they enjoy their vehicle.

Underpinning our Post-Purchase Support Initiative is our commitment to quality service. Our service centers, staffed with factory-trained technicians, use only genuine parts to guarantee the quality of any repairs or maintenance work. This ensures that all vehicles are maintained according to the manufacturer's standards and perform optimally.

You are an expert in Salesforce and you have access to a Salesforce instance. You are interacting with the system and a human user (i.e., a customer).

# Instructions
- You will be provided a question, the system description, and relevant task context.
- You MUST follow the Plan-and-Solve approach:
  1. PLAN: First, analyze the question and devise a clear step-by-step plan to solve it. Identify what information you need and in what order.
  2. SOLVE: Then, execute each step of your plan one at a time using SOQL/SOSL queries.
- Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) and Salesforce Object Search Language (SOSL) can be used to query the system.
- Your generation should always contain a structured output and NOTHING ELSE. Generate only one action per turn.
- DO NOT generate ANY system observation, you will receive this based on your Action command.
- If multiple records are found matching the requirements mentioned, just return a comma-separated string.
- If no record is found matching the requirements mentioned, just return 'None'.

# Output Format

## On your FIRST turn, create a plan:
<plan>
Step 1: [describe what you need to find first]
Step 2: [describe the next piece of information needed]
Step 3: [describe how to combine/compute the answer]
...
</plan>
<thought> Starting with Step 1: [brief reasoning] </thought>
<execute> a valid SOQL/SOSL query for Step 1 </execute>

## On SUBSEQUENT turns, execute the next step:
<thought> [which step you are on, what you learned, what to do next] </thought>
<execute> a valid SOQL/SOSL query </execute>

## When you have enough information to answer:
<thought> [summarize findings and confirm the answer] </thought>
<respond> final answer </respond>

# Guidelines
- Always start with a plan on your first turn. Keep your plan SHORT — ideally 2-4 steps. Avoid over-planning.
- Track which step of the plan you are executing.
- If a step's result changes your plan, briefly note the adjustment in your thought.
- Execute SOQL/SOSL queries to gather information step by step.
- IMPORTANT: You MUST submit a <respond> action with your best answer before running out of turns. If you have partial information, respond with your best guess rather than continuing to search.
- Always end with a <respond> action containing ONLY the answer — a short phrase or value. NO full sentences, NO paraphrasing, NO explanation.

# Example
Question: What is the total number of opportunities?
Output (Turn 1):
<plan>
Step 1: Query the total count of Opportunity records
</plan>
<thought> Starting with Step 1: I need to count all opportunities. </thought>
<execute> SELECT COUNT() FROM Opportunity </execute>

Output (Turn 2):
<thought> The count is 100. Plan complete. </thought>
<respond> 100 </respond>

User

  - Email: Email address of the agent

  - FirstName: First name of the agent
  - Id: ID of the user
  - LanguageLocaleKey: Language locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - LastName: Last name of the agent
  - LocaleSidKey: Locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - Phone: Phone number of the agent
  - ProfileId: ID of the user profile, typically 'Standard User' or a similar profile

  - Description: Detailed description of the case
  - Id: ID of the case
  - IssueId__c: ID of the Issue associated with this case

Knowledge__kav

  - UrlName: Unique URL for the article
ProductCategory
  - CatalogId: ID of the catalog this category belongs to

Product2

  - Name: Name of the product

traj.content:
You are an expert in Salesforce and you have access to a Salesforce instance. You are interacting with the system and a human user (i.e., a customer).

# Instructions
- You will be provided a question, the system description, and relevant task context.
- You MUST follow the Plan-and-Solve approach:
  1. PLAN: First, analyze the question and devise a clear step-by-step plan to solve it. Identify what information you need and in what order.
  2. SOLVE: Then, execute each step of your plan one at a time using SOQL/SOSL queries.
- Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) and Salesforce Object Search Language (SOSL) can be used to query the system.
- Your generation should always contain a structured output and NOTHING ELSE. Generate only one action per turn.
- DO NOT generate ANY system observation, you will receive this based on your Action command.
- If multiple records are found matching the requirements mentioned, just return a comma-separated string.
- If no record is found matching the requirements mentioned, just return 'None'.

# Output Format

## On your FIRST turn, create a plan:
<plan>
Step 1: [describe what you need to find first]
Step 2: [describe the next piece of information needed]
Step 3: [describe how to combine/compute the answer]
...
</plan>
<thought> Starting with Step 1: [brief reasoning] </thought>
<execute> a valid SOQL/SOSL query for Step 1 </execute>

## On SUBSEQUENT turns, execute the next step:
<thought> [which step you are on, what you learned, what to do next] </thought>
<execute> a valid SOQL/SOSL query </execute>

## When you have enough information to answer:
<thought> [summarize findings and confirm the answer] </thought>
<respond> final answer </respond>

# Guidelines
- Always start with a plan on your first turn. Keep your plan SHORT — ideally 2-4 steps. Avoid over-planning.
- Track which step of the plan you are executing.
- If a step's result changes your plan, briefly note the adjustment in your thought.
- Execute SOQL/SOSL queries to gather information step by step.
- IMPORTANT: You MUST submit a <respond> action with your best answer before running out of turns. If you have partial information, respond with your best guess rather than continuing to search.
- Always end with a <respond> action containing ONLY the answer — a short phrase or value. NO full sentences, NO paraphrasing, NO explanation.

# Example
Question: What is the total number of opportunities?
Output (Turn 1):
<plan>
Step 1: Query the total count of Opportunity records
</plan>
<thought> Starting with Step 1: I need to count all opportunities. </thought>
<execute> SELECT COUNT() FROM Opportunity </execute>

Output (Turn 2):
<thought> The count is 100. Plan complete. </thought>
<respond> 100 </respond>

# Salesforce instance description
The objects available in the Salesforce instance are:
User, Account, Contact, Case, Knowledge__kav, ProductCategory, Product2

## The fields available for the objects along with their descriptions and dependencies are:
User
  - Alias: Alias, usually a shortened version of the agent's name
  - Email: Email address of the agent
  - EmailEncodingKey: Email encoding, usually 'UTF-8'
  - FirstName: First name of the agent
  - Id: ID of the user
  - LanguageLocaleKey: Language locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - LastName: Last name of the agent
  - LocaleSidKey: Locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - Phone: Phone number of the agent
  - ProfileId: ID of the user profile, typically 'Standard User' or a similar profile
  - TimeZoneSidKey: Time zone, e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles'
  - Username: Unique username for the agent in Salesforce
Account
  - Description: Detailed description of the account
  - FirstName: First name of the account
  - Id: ID of the account
  - LastName: Last name of the account
  - Name: Name of the account
  - PersonEmail: Email address of the person associated with the account
  - Phone: Phone number of the account
  - ShippingState: State for shipping address
Contact
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - Id: ID of the contact
Case
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - ClosedDate: Date when the case was closed in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - ContactId: ID of the contact associated with this case. References Contact object
  - CreatedDate: Date when the case was created in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Description: Detailed description of the case
  - Id: ID of the case
  - IssueId__c: ID of the Issue associated with this case
  - OrderItemId__c: ID of the Order Item associated with this case
  - OwnerId: The latest owner of the case. References User object
  - Priority: Priority of the case
  - Status: Status of the case, e.g., 'New', 'Working', 'Escalated', etc
  - Subject: Subject of the case
Knowledge__kav
  - FAQ_Answer__c: Content of the knowledge article
  - Id: ID of the knowledge article
  - Summary: Summary of the article
  - Title: Title of the knowledge article
  - UrlName: Unique URL for the article
ProductCategory
  - CatalogId: ID of the catalog this category belongs to
  - Id: ID of the product category
  - Name: Name of the product category
Product2
  - Description: Description of the product
  - External_ID__c: External ID for mapping purposes
  - Id: ID of the product
  - IsActive: Boolean to mark if the product is active
  - Name: Name of the product



# Task-Specific Guidance
For questions about product features, policies, warranties, payment plans, financing, terms, or company knowledge, query the `Knowledge__kav` object.
Fields: `Id`, `Title`, `Summary`, `FAQ_Answer__c` (article content).
SOSL search: FIND {keyword} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Knowledge__kav(Id, Title, FAQ_Answer__c)
SOSL RULES: Inside FIND {}, do NOT use quotes, hyphens, or special characters. Use only simple words separated by spaces. Example: FIND {tire maintenance} not FIND {"tire-maintenance"}.
Your <respond> must be a SHORT phrase extracted from the article. NO full sentences or paraphrasing.# Additional task context
- Use the information retrieved from the knowledge articles to answer the question in a concise manner
How does AutoElite Motors ensure smooth resolution of technology issues?

<plan>
Step 1: Search for knowledge articles related to technology issue resolution.
</plan>

In addition to assessment, AutoElite Motors emphasizes preventive care by providing guidance on maintaining a battery-friendly driving environment. For instance, minimizing short trips and ensuring the battery is fully charged can significantly extend its life. Given that batteries naturally lose power over time and extreme conditions exacerbate this loss, our technicians may recommend proactive steps or even timely replacement to avoid inconvenience.

Moreover, our service centers are equipped with genuine battery replacements that meet the high standards of performance and reliability our customers expect. By choosing a genuine battery, our clients can rest assured that their vehicle's performance will remain uncompromised, maintaining the quality and reliability synonymous with AutoElite Motors.

This commitment is reflected in the latest sustainable offerings available at our dealerships, providing customers with choices that are not only better for the planet but also innovative in design and functionality.

traj.content:
You are an expert in Salesforce and you have access to a Salesforce instance. You are interacting with the system and a human user (i.e., a customer).

# Instructions
- You will be provided a question, the system description, and relevant task context.
- You MUST follow the Plan-and-Solve approach:
  1. PLAN: First, analyze the question and devise a clear step-by-step plan to solve it. Identify what information you need and in what order.
  2. SOLVE: Then, execute each step of your plan one at a time using SOQL/SOSL queries.
- Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) and Salesforce Object Search Language (SOSL) can be used to query the system.
- Your generation should always contain a structured output and NOTHING ELSE. Generate only one action per turn.
- DO NOT generate ANY system observation, you will receive this based on your Action command.
- If multiple records are found matching the requirements mentioned, just return a comma-separated string.
- If no record is found matching the requirements mentioned, just return 'None'.

# Output Format

## On your FIRST turn, create a plan:
<plan>
Step 1: [describe what you need to find first]
Step 2: [describe the next piece of information needed]
Step 3: [describe how to combine/compute the answer]
...
</plan>
<thought> Starting with Step 1: [brief reasoning] </thought>
<execute> a valid SOQL/SOSL query for Step 1 </execute>

## On SUBSEQUENT turns, execute the next step:
<thought> [which step you are on, what you learned, what to do next] </thought>
<execute> a valid SOQL/SOSL query </execute>

## When you have enough information to answer:
<thought> [summarize findings and confirm the answer] </thought>
<respond> final answer </respond>

# Guidelines
- Always start with a plan on your first turn. Keep your plan SHORT — ideally 2-4 steps. Avoid over-planning.
- Track which step of the plan you are executing.
- If a step's result changes your plan, briefly note the adjustment in your thought.
- Execute SOQL/SOSL queries to gather information step by step.
- IMPORTANT: You MUST submit a <respond> action with your best answer before running out of turns. If you have partial information, respond with your best guess rather than continuing to search.
- Always end with a <respond> action containing ONLY the answer — a short phrase or value. NO full sentences, NO paraphrasing, NO explanation.

# Example
Question: What is the total number of opportunities?
Output (Turn 1):
<plan>
Step 1: Query the total count of Opportunity records
</plan>
<thought> Starting with Step 1: I need to count all opportunities. </thought>
<execute> SELECT COUNT() FROM Opportunity </execute>

Output (Turn 2):
<thought> The count is 100. Plan complete. </thought>
<respond> 100 </respond>

# Salesforce instance description
The objects available in the Salesforce instance are:
User, Account, Contact, Case, Knowledge__kav, ProductCategory, Product2

## The fields available for the objects along with their descriptions and dependencies are:
User
  - Alias: Alias, usually a shortened version of the agent's name
  - Email: Email address of the agent
  - EmailEncodingKey: Email encoding, usually 'UTF-8'
  - FirstName: First name of the agent
  - Id: ID of the user
  - LanguageLocaleKey: Language locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - LastName: Last name of the agent
  - LocaleSidKey: Locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - Phone: Phone number of the agent
  - ProfileId: ID of the user profile, typically 'Standard User' or a similar profile
  - TimeZoneSidKey: Time zone, e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles'
  - Username: Unique username for the agent in Salesforce
Account
  - Description: Detailed description of the account
  - FirstName: First name of the account
  - Id: ID of the account
  - LastName: Last name of the account
  - Name: Name of the account
  - PersonEmail: Email address of the person associated with the account
  - Phone: Phone number of the account
  - ShippingState: State for shipping address
Contact
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - Id: ID of the contact
Case
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - ClosedDate: Date when the case was closed in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - ContactId: ID of the contact associated with this case. References Contact object
  - CreatedDate: Date when the case was created in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Description: Detailed description of the case
  - Id: ID of the case
  - IssueId__c: ID of the Issue associated with this case
  - OrderItemId__c: ID of the Order Item associated with this case
  - OwnerId: The latest owner of the case. References User object
  - Priority: Priority of the case
  - Status: Status of the case, e.g., 'New', 'Working', 'Escalated', etc
  - Subject: Subject of the case
Knowledge__kav
  - FAQ_Answer__c: Content of the knowledge article
  - Id: ID of the knowledge article
  - Summary: Summary of the article
  - Title: Title of the knowledge article
  - UrlName: Unique URL for the article
ProductCategory
  - CatalogId: ID of the catalog this category belongs to
  - Id: ID of the product category
  - Name: Name of the product category
Product2
  - Description: Description of the product
  - External_ID__c: External ID for mapping purposes
  - Id: ID of the product
  - IsActive: Boolean to mark if the product is active
  - Name: Name of the product



# Task-Specific Guidance
For questions about product features, policies, warranties, payment plans, financing, terms, or company knowledge, query the `Knowledge__kav` object.
Fields: `Id`, `Title`, `Summary`, `FAQ_Answer__c` (article content).
SOSL search: FIND {keyword} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Knowledge__kav(Id, Title, FAQ_Answer__c)
SOSL RULES: Inside FIND {}, do NOT use quotes, hyphens, or special characters. Use only simple words separated by spaces. Example: FIND {tire maintenance} not FIND {"tire-maintenance"}.
Your <respond> must be a SHORT phrase extracted from the article. NO full sentences or paraphrasing.# Additional task context
- Use the information retrieved from the knowledge articles to answer the question in a concise manner
How long after a test drive incident can customers avail the Enhanced Detailing Service?

<plan>
Step 1: Search for knowledge articles related to 'test drive incident' and 'Enhanced Detailing Service'.
</plan>
<thought> Starting with Step 1: I need to find relevant knowledge articles that discuss the timeframe for availing Enhanced Detailing Service after a test drive incident. </thought>
<execute> FIND {test drive incident Enhanced Detailing Service} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Knowledge__kav(Id, Title, FAQ_Answer__c) </execute>

Salesforce instance output: [{'Id': 'ka0Ws000000pyq9IAA', 'Title': 'Ensuring Seamless Test Drive Experiences at AutoElite Motors', 'FAQ_Answer__c': "At AutoElite Motors, we are committed to delivering an exceptional car-buying experience for our customers. To address this issue and enhance our customer experience, AutoElite Motors has implemented two primary solutions: Priority Appointment Rescheduling and Guaranteed Test Drive Slot.

The first solution, Priority Appointment Rescheduling, ensures that any customer who encounters a test drive unavailability has the opportunity to reschedule promptly. This proactive measure helps maintain customer trust and ensures consistent satisfaction, reinforcing our dedication to seamless service.

In addition to rescheduling flexibility, AutoElite Motors introduces the Guaranteed Test Drive Slot program. This solution offers customers a reservation system that securely confirms their test drive time, explicitly designed to avoid scheduling conflicts and ensure vehicle availability. With a window of validation stretching up to 30 days prior to the scheduled appointment, the Guaranteed Test Drive Slot accommodates dynamic scheduling needs, offering peace of mind and fostering reliability in our services. By confirming a secured time for each test drive, this program eliminates uncertainties and underscores our commitment to providing a top-tier, hassle-free experience.

Through these solutions, AutoElite Motors aims to not only address the immediate issue of test drive availability but also enhance our operational processes. Our focus remains on transparent communication, meticulous planning, and delivering unparalleled satisfaction to everyone who walks through our doors."}, {'Id': 'ka0Ws000000q0K5IAI', 'Title': 'Preserving Vehicle Aesthetics: Detailing Services by AutoElite Motors', 'FAQ_Answer__c': "Maintaining the aesthetic appeal of your vehicle is just as crucial as ensuring its mechanical performance. The exterior treatment involves meticulous cleaning, polishing, and sealing processes that work to protect your vehicle against environmental elements. We use high-performance waxes and sealants that not only enhance the gloss and color depth of your vehicle's paint but also provide a protective barrier against UV rays, dirt, and debris, ensuring your vehicle remains in superior condition.

We also pay close attention to every detail of your vehicle's interior to maintain a clean and comfortable environment. Our interior detailing services include deep cleaning and conditioning of all upholstery, carpets, and mats. Leather surfaces receive specialized care with premium conditioners that prevent cracking and wear while maintaining softness and enhancing their natural luster. Similarly, plastic and vinyl surfaces are treated with conditioners that prevent fading and aging, ensuring they look as good as new.

Additionally, AutoElite Motors' detailing services encompass meticulous attention to wheels and tires. Our technicians delicately clean and condition wheels while applying a protective coating that enhances shine and guards against road grime and brake dust. Tire dressing adds the finishing touch, delivering a deep, rich color that complements the sleek appearance of your vehicle.

Beyond the regular detailing offerings, we provide specialized services like ceramic coating applications, which offer advanced protection and exceptional gloss that can last for years. Our ceramic coatings provide a durable shield against contaminants and reduces maintenance requirements, making it easier for you to keep your vehicle looking its best without extensive effort.

AutoElite Motors' detailing services not only enhance your vehicle's aesthetic appeal but also contribute to maintaining its value and extending its lifespan. By combining expert craftsmanship with top-tier products, we ensure your vehicle reflects the pride you take in ownership and continues to impress every time you hit the road. With AutoElite Motors, preserving your vehicle's aesthetics means enjoying a like-new appearance that complements the premium driving experience we are synonymous with."}, {'Id': 'ka0Ws000000pyzpIAA', 'Title': 'Resolving Online Inventory Discrepancies at AutoElite Motors', 'FAQ_Answer__c': "Resolving online inventory discrepancies is an essential part of maintaining customer trust and satisfaction at AutoElite Motors. You are an expert in Salesforce and you have access to a Salesforce instance. You are interacting with the system and a human user (i.e., a customer).

# Instructions
- You will be provided a question, the system description, and relevant task context.
- You MUST follow the Plan-and-Solve approach:
  1. PLAN: First, analyze the question and devise a clear step-by-step plan to solve it. Identify what information you need and in what order.
  2. SOLVE: Then, execute each step of your plan one at a time using SOQL/SOSL queries.
- Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) and Salesforce Object Search Language (SOSL) can be used to query the system.
- Your generation should always contain a structured output and NOTHING ELSE. Generate only one action per turn.
- DO NOT generate ANY system observation, you will receive this based on your Action command.
- If multiple records are found matching the requirements mentioned, just return a comma-separated string.
- If no record is found matching the requirements mentioned, just return 'None'.

# Output Format

## On your FIRST turn, create a plan:
<plan>
Step 1: [describe what you need to find first]
Step 2: [describe the next piece of information needed]
Step 3: [describe how to combine/compute the answer]
...
</plan>
<thought> Starting with Step 1: [brief reasoning] </thought>
<execute> a valid SOQL/SOSL query for Step 1 </execute>

## On SUBSEQUENT turns, execute the next step:
<thought> [which step you are on, what you learned, what to do next] </thought>
<execute> a valid SOQL/SOSL query </execute>

## When you have enough information to answer:
<thought> [summarize findings and confirm the answer] </thought>
<respond> final answer </respond>

# Guidelines
- Always start with a plan on your first turn. Keep your plan SHORT — ideally 2-4 steps. Avoid over-planning.
- Track which step of the plan you are executing.
- If a step's result changes your plan, briefly note the adjustment in your thought.
- Execute SOQL/SOSL queries to gather information step by step.
- IMPORTANT: You MUST submit a <respond> action with your best answer before running out of turns. If you have partial information, respond with your best guess rather than continuing to search.
- Always end with a <respond> action containing ONLY the answer — a short phrase or value. NO full sentences, NO paraphrasing, NO explanation.

# Example
Question: What is the total number of opportunities?
Output (Turn 1):
<plan>
Step 1: Query the total count of Opportunity records
</plan>
<thought> Starting with Step 1: I need to count all opportunities. </thought>
<execute> SELECT COUNT() FROM Opportunity </execute>

Output (Turn 2):

<respond> 100 </respond>

# Salesforce instance description
The objects available in the Salesforce instance are:
User, Account, Contact, Case, Knowledge__kav, ProductCategory, Product2

## The fields available for the objects along with their descriptions and dependencies are:
User
  - Alias: Alias, usually a shortened version of the agent's name
  - Email: Email address of the agent
  - EmailEncodingKey: Email encoding, usually 'UTF-8'
  - FirstName: First name of the agent
  - Id: ID of the user
  - LanguageLocaleKey: Language locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - LastName: Last name of the agent
  - LocaleSidKey: Locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - Phone: Phone number of the agent
  - ProfileId: ID of the user profile, typically 'Standard User' or a similar profile
  - TimeZoneSidKey: Time zone, e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles'
  - Username: Unique username for the agent in Salesforce
Account
  - Description: Detailed description of the account
  - FirstName: First name of the account
  - Id: ID of the account
  - LastName: Last name of the account
  - Name: Name of the account
  - PersonEmail: Email address of the person associated with the account
  - Phone: Phone number of the account
  - ShippingState: State for shipping address
Contact
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - Id: ID of the contact
Case
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - ClosedDate: Date when the case was closed in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - ContactId: ID of the contact associated with this case. References Contact object
  - CreatedDate: Date when the case was created in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Description: Detailed description of the case
  - Id: ID of the case
  - IssueId__c: ID of the Issue associated with this case
  - OrderItemId__c: ID of the Order Item associated with this case
  - OwnerId: The latest owner of the case. References User object
  - Priority: Priority of the case
  - Status: Status of the case, e.g., 'New', 'Working', 'Escalated', etc
  - Subject: Subject of the case
Knowledge__kav
  - FAQ_Answer__c: Content of the knowledge article
  - Id: ID of the knowledge article
  - Summary: Summary of the article
  - Title: Title of the knowledge article
  - UrlName: Unique URL for the article
ProductCategory
  - CatalogId: ID of the catalog this category belongs to
  - Id: ID of the product category
  - Name: Name of the product category
Product2
  - Description: Description of the product
  - External_ID__c: External ID for mapping purposes
  - Id: ID of the product
  - IsActive: Boolean to mark if the product is active
  - Name: Name of the product

Salesforce instance output: [{'Id': 'ka0Ws000000pyqBIAQ', 'Title': "Mastering Pre-Owned Luxury: AutoElite Motors' Selection Process", 'FAQ_Answer__c': 'At AutoElite Motors, our Certified Pre-Owned (CPO) program is designed to provide our customers with high-quality pre-owned luxury vehicles at an accessible price. We understand the allure of luxury, but we also recognize the importance of value, reliability, and performance, especially when it comes to pre-owned vehicles. That’s why we have established a stringent selection process to ensure that every pre-owned vehicle we offer meets our premium standards, focusing on quality and luxury.

The process begins with handpicking vehicles based on their condition, mileage, and age. We meticulously evaluate each vehicle to ensure it has a clean title and no major accident history. Once selected, every vehicle undergoes a rigorous 150-point inspection conducted by our factory-trained technicians. This thorough inspection process involves checking every component, from the engine to the electrical systems, ensuring the vehicle’s safety, reliability, and performance. Any issues identified during the inspection are addressed with repairs and replacements using genuine parts.

Our commitment to providing luxury quality doesn’t stop at the inspection. Each certified pre-owned vehicle comes with extended warranty coverage, which includes comprehensive protection against unforeseen repairs, offering peace of mind similar to that of a brand-new car. Beyond mechanical aspects, our CPO program also offers customers the luxury experience through premium features, which might include high-end interiors, advanced technology, and modern safety systems, without the sticker shock of buying new.

Moreover, AutoElite Motors extends flexible financing solutions to support the purchase of certified pre-owned luxury vehicles. This includes customized finance plans that accommodate different budgetary requirements, making luxury more attainable for a broader range of customers. In addition, our dedicated customer support team is always ready to assist, ensuring an effortless experience from initial inquiry through ownership and servicing. You are an expert in Salesforce and you have access to a Salesforce instance. You are interacting with the system and a human user (i.e., a customer).

# Instructions
- You will be provided a question, the system description, and relevant task context.
- You MUST follow the Plan-and-Solve approach:
  1. PLAN: First, analyze the question and devise a clear step-by-step plan to solve it. Identify what information you need and in what order.
  2. SOLVE: Then, execute each step of your plan one at a time using SOQL/SOSL queries.
- Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) and Salesforce Object Search Language (SOSL) can be used to query the system.
- Your generation should always contain a structured output and NOTHING ELSE. Generate only one action per turn.
- DO NOT generate ANY system observation, you will receive this based on your Action command.
- If multiple records are found matching the requirements mentioned, just return a comma-separated string.
- If no record is found matching the requirements mentioned, just return 'None'.

# Output Format

## On your FIRST turn, create a plan:
<plan>
Step 1: [describe what you need to find first]
Step 2: [describe the next piece of information needed]
Step 3: [describe how to combine/compute the answer]
...
</plan>
<thought> Starting with Step 1: [brief reasoning] </thought>
<execute> a valid SOQL/SOSL query for Step 1 </execute>

## On SUBSEQUENT turns, execute the next step:
<thought> [which step you are on, what you learned, what to do next] </thought>
<execute> a valid SOQL/SOSL query </execute>

## When you have enough information to answer:
<thought> [summarize findings and confirm the answer] </thought>
<respond> final answer </respond>

# Guidelines
- Always start with a plan on your first turn. Keep your plan SHORT — ideally 2-4 steps. Avoid over-planning.
- Track which step of the plan you are executing.
- If a step's result changes your plan, briefly note the adjustment in your thought.
- Execute SOQL/SOSL queries to gather information step by step.
- IMPORTANT: You MUST submit a <respond> action with your best answer before running out of turns. If you have partial information, respond with your best guess rather than continuing to search.
- Always end with a <respond> action containing ONLY the answer — a short phrase or value. NO full sentences, NO paraphrasing, NO explanation.

# Example
Question: What is the total number of opportunities?
Output (Turn 1):
<plan>
Step 1: Query the total count of Opportunity records
</plan>
<thought> Starting with Step 1: I need to count all opportunities. </thought>
<execute> SELECT COUNT() FROM Opportunity </execute>

Output (Turn 2):
<thought> The count is 100. Plan complete. </thought>
<respond> 100 </respond>

# Salesforce instance description
The objects available in the Salesforce instance are:
User, Account, Contact, Case, Knowledge__kav, ProductCategory, Product2

## The fields available for the objects along with their descriptions and dependencies are:
User
  - Alias: Alias, usually a shortened version of the agent's name
  - Email: Email address of the agent
  - EmailEncodingKey: Email encoding, usually 'UTF-8'
  - FirstName: First name of the agent
  - Id: ID of the user
  - LanguageLocaleKey: Language locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - LastName: Last name of the agent
  - LocaleSidKey: Locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - Phone: Phone number of the agent
  - ProfileId: ID of the user profile, typically 'Standard User' or a similar profile
  - TimeZoneSidKey: Time zone, e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles'
  - Username: Unique username for the agent in Salesforce
Account
  - Description: Detailed description of the account
  - FirstName: First name of the account
  - Id: ID of the account
  - LastName: Last name of the account
  - Name: Name of the account
  - PersonEmail: Email address of the person associated with the account
  - Phone: Phone number of the account
  - ShippingState: State for shipping address
Contact
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - Id: ID of the contact
Case
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - ClosedDate: Date when the case was closed in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - ContactId: ID of the contact associated with this case. References Contact object
  - CreatedDate: Date when the case was created in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Description: Detailed description of the case
  - Id: ID of the case
  - IssueId__c: ID of the Issue associated with this case
  - OrderItemId__c: ID of the Order Item associated with this case
  - OwnerId: The latest owner of the case. References User object
  - Priority: Priority of the case
  - Status: Status of the case, e.g., 'New', 'Working', 'Escalated', etc
  - Subject: Subject of the case
Knowledge__kav
  - FAQ_Answer__c: Content of the knowledge article
  - Id: ID of the knowledge article
  - Summary: Summary of the article
  - Title: Title of the knowledge article
  - UrlName: Unique URL for the article
ProductCategory
  - CatalogId: ID of the catalog this category belongs to
  - Id: ID of the product category
  - Name: Name of the product category
Product2
  - Description: Description of the product
  - External_ID__c: External ID for mapping purposes
  - Id: ID of the product
  - IsActive: Boolean to mark if the product is active
  - Name: Name of the product

Our comprehensive brake servicing options are designed to enhance stopping power and ensure your safety on the road. Whether you're driving a brand-new luxury model or a reliable pre-owned vehicle, maintaining high-performance braking systems is crucial for optimal vehicle handling and safety.

Our brake services begin with a detailed inspection conducted by our factory-trained technicians. This inspection covers all components of your brake system, including brake pads, rotors, calipers, and brake fluid levels. Using state-of-the-art diagnostic tools, our technicians can accurately assess the condition of your brakes, identifying any wear and tear or potential issues that may affect performance.

At AutoElite Motors, we believe in using only high-quality materials. During our brake servicing, we replace worn-out brake pads with premium options that deliver superior stopping power and longevity. Rotors are carefully examined and resurfaced or replaced as necessary to ensure smooth and consistent braking performance. The use of genuine parts and materials means that your brakes are engineered to perform as the manufacturer intended.

Brake fluid is another critical component of your vehicle's braking system. Over time, brake fluid can become contaminated with moisture, reducing its ability to transfer pressure effectively. Our technicians check the condition of the brake fluid and replace it with high-quality brake fluid as needed, ensuring that your braking system remains responsive and reliable.

In addition to regular brake servicing, AutoElite Motors offers performance upgrades for those seeking enhanced braking capabilities. Whether you require increased braking power for performance driving or are looking for quieter operation, our selection of performance brake options can be tailored to meet your specific needs.

Our dedication to transparency and customer satisfaction extends to every aspect of our brake services. We provide clear explanations of any findings during inspections and offer recommendations for service based on your vehicle's requirements. With competitive pricing and flexible service schedules, maintaining high-performance braking systems at AutoElite Motors is both convenient and cost-effective.

By choosing AutoElite Motors for your brake servicing needs, you are ensuring that your vehicle's braking system receives the expert attention and high-quality materials it deserves. Prioritizing brake performance not only enhances vehicle safety but also contributes to a smoother, more controlled driving experience. Trust AutoElite Motors to keep your brakes in top condition, so you can feel confident and secure every time you hit the road."}, {'Id': 'ka0Ws000000q0APIAY', 'Title': 'AutoElite Motors: Ensuring Reliable Vehicle Performance', 'FAQ_Answer__c': "The performance and safety of a vehicle are paramount concerns for any car owner. At AutoElite Motors, we place great emphasis on ensuring that every vehicle, whether new or pre-owned, runs reliably and safely. Regular check-ups at AutoElite Motors involve detailed inspections carried out by our factory-trained technicians. During routine maintenance visits, technicians perform a thorough evaluation of critical components such as brakes, steering, suspension, and tires. This preventive approach helps in maintaining optimal vehicle performance and ensures that any potential safety risks are mitigated timely.

Our dealerships offer competitive maintenance plans that can be tailored to meet your specific driving needs and schedules. Whether you are using your vehicle for daily commutes or long-haul drives, our maintenance plans are designed to keep your car in peak condition, thereby enhancing its overall value and lifespan.

In addition to maintaining your vehicle's performance, regular check-ups often lead to better fuel efficiency, reduced emissions, and improved longevity, all of which contribute to lower operating costs. By choosing AutoElite Motors for your maintenance needs, you are ensuring that your vehicle will not only perform efficiently but also remain a safe and reliable companion on the road for years to come.

At AutoElite Motors, our goal is to make sure your car-buying experience transitions seamlessly into car ownership. With our unwavering focus on preventive maintenance and safety enhancements, you can drive with confidence knowing that your vehicle's performance and safety are being professionally managed."}, {'Id': 'ka0Ws000000q0ITIAY', 'Title': 'Ensuring Optimal Engine Performance with AutoElite Motors', 'FAQ_Answer__c': "Ensuring optimal eng
